message:
fix(ft-api): gaussian_nll_loss + focal_loss autograd composition; close [bd-2med]

Closes the second-batch loss-severance umbrella. focal_loss
(used in dense object detection / RetinaNet) and gaussian_nll_loss
(variational / probabilistic regression) used to extract values,
compute the per-element formula in plain f64, build a non-grad
leaf, then take tensor_mean. Same severed-autograd pattern as the
first-batch losses (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a / x9cr / zl3n).

gaussian_nll_loss [bd-vjow]:
  Compose through clamp_min/log/sub/mul/div/mean primitives.
  All three of input / target / var are differentiable. Added
  gaussian_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_var
  regression test: input=2, target=1, var=4 → analytical
  grad_input = (input - target) / var / n = 0.25,
  grad_var = 0.5 * (1/var - (input-target)^2 / var^2) / n = 0.09375.

focal_loss [bd-m6uk]:
  Compose through sigmoid + linear-blend-by-target + pow + log +
  mean. Replaced the per-element target conditionals with linear
  blends:
    p_t      = target * p + (1 - target) * (1 - p)
    alpha_t  = target * alpha + (1 - target) * (1 - alpha)
  Added focal_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input sanity
  regression test (finite + non-zero on both entries; the
  closed-form for gamma > 0 is messy so we avoid asserting exact
  values).

Closes frankentorch-vjow / frankentorch-m6uk / frankentorch-2med.
ft-api lib tests: 1159 passing (+2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-1s2","title":"Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes","description":"Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:49:10.441649598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804652781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804379905Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1tax","title":"Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp","description":"These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.","notes":"All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:24:23.438424203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.517650830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.515232671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-2med","title":"Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll","description":"Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.","notes":"All children closed: poisson_nll (zl3n, 8d33f51), gaussian_nll (vjow, this commit), focal_loss (m6uk, this commit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:54.140379643Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:54.137798514Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:54.437563761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045687331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045431557Z","close_reason":"slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2rl","title":"Wire f32 tensor comparisons and float classify to dispatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:57:24.571301561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720640114Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720320760Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2xxj","title":"Activation functions lack subprocess conformance: sigmoid, tanh, silu, mish","description":"Existing subprocess conformance suite covers gelu, softplus, exp/log/expm1/log1p/erf/erfc/lgamma/trig/hyperbolic, but the workhorse activations sigmoid / tanh / silu / mish have no scipy or libm reference comparison. PyTorch parity for these matters because:
- sigmoid (= 1/(1+exp(-x)) = expit) is the building block for binary classification, gating, attention
- tanh is the workhorse for RNN/LSTM
- silu/swish (= x*sigmoid(x)) is used in transformer FFN
- mish (= x*tanh(softplus(x))) is used in detection / vision

Reference: scipy.special.expit (sigmoid), numpy.tanh, numpy compositions for silu and mish. ULP-tolerant comparison; FrankenTorch composes these through libm exp/tanh which should match within ~1 ULP on smooth interior plus edge cases for ±inf / NaN / large-magnitude saturation.

Add torch_activations_libm_subprocess_conformance covering all four with ~30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / large / Inf / NaN. Filed during testing-conformance pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Added torch_activations_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering sigmoid (vs scipy.special.expit), tanh (vs numpy.tanh), silu = x*sigmoid(x), and mish = x*tanh(softplus(x)) across 30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / saturation (±50, ±100, ±700) / Inf / NaN. ULP-tolerant comparator: 4 ULP for sigmoid/tanh, 16 ULP for the composed silu/mish. Subprocess conformance count now 33 (was 32).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:09:45.897091490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.783694467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.780442649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-ltv","title":"ft-api: validate multi_margin_loss target indices","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:05:08.686852608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:08:25.273168072Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:08:25.272894114Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-m6uk","title":"Fix focal_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]","description":"focal_loss at lib.rs:10324 computes elementwise:
  p     = sigmoid(input)
  p_t   = p if target==1 else 1-p
  alpha_t = alpha if target==1 else 1-alpha
  loss  = -alpha_t * (1-p_t)^gamma * log(p_t.max(1e-15))

Composable through autograd primitives. The target-conditional branches can be replaced by linear blends with target as a {0, 1} mask:
  p_t       = target * p + (1 - target) * (1 - p)
  alpha_t   = target * alpha + (1 - target) * (1 - alpha)   (multiplied via a target-derived non-grad scalar tensor)
  inner     = (1 - p_t)^gamma * log(clamp_min(p_t, 1e-15))
  loss_per_elem = -alpha_t * inner
  loss      = mean(per_elem)

Used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.). Last child of frankentorch-2med.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed focal_loss through sigmoid + linear-blend-by-target + pow + log + mean. Replaced the per-element conditionals with linear blends: p_t = target * p + (1 - target) * (1 - p), alpha_t = target * alpha + (1 - target) * (1 - alpha). Added focal_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (sanity finite + non-zero check, since the focal closed-form for gamma > 0 is messy).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:43:34.823895024Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:53.860009331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:53.857676522Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-max","title":"Guard bincount/histc for oversized inputs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:27:02.896521943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:27:04.870803910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:27:04.870554247Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-mbl","title":"Flaky ft-conformance::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam (~0.22 vs 0.2 threshold)","description":"ft-conformance::tests::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam reproducibly fails on a clean main checkout (verified at HEAD=fcad235 prior to 2026-04-27 work, and again at HEAD=dd9ae7b after).

Failure mode: 'prediction -4.779605595282768 should stay close to target -5' — the |pred - target| < 0.2 threshold at crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:12780 fails by ~0.02 on a single element. The test trains 80 steps with Adam through a custom autograd function layer and asserts MSE-style convergence.

Reproducibility: deterministic — same numeric value on every run on the rch fleet. Suggests stochastic init not properly seeded OR the convergence threshold is just slightly too tight for the configured Adam lr/iter budget.

Suggested fix paths (any one is fine):
1. Seed the weight init explicitly via session.set_seed(...) and tune the threshold to actual convergence.
2. Increase iteration budget from 80 to 200 if accuracy matters.
3. Loosen the per-element threshold from 0.2 to 0.25 with a comment explaining the bound.

Not blocking shipped work — all touched paths still pass; this is a pre-existing test-suite stability issue surfaced during the 2026-04-27 reality check.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:31.545542241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:28:00.779338946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:28:00.777618866Z","close_reason":"Bumped Adam iteration budget from 80 to 200 in custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam — 80 was right at the convergence boundary (worst element |delta|=0.22 vs 0.2 threshold) so the test failed deterministically on a clean main checkout. 200 gives Adam comfortable headroom; full ft-conformance suite (88 tests) passes; no clippy/ubs regressions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-myj","title":"Fail closed on invalid functional dropout probabilities and embedding indices","description":"ft-api functional helpers currently accept invalid inputs too permissively: functional_dropout does not reject p<0 or p>1, and functional_embedding lossy-casts float indices to usize instead of rejecting negative or fractional values. Tighten these APIs to explicit fail-closed behavior and add targeted tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:30.628000763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T14:19:39.422034110Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T14:19:39.421762196Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Actual members include ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and ft-conformance/ft-runtime responsibilities are broader than the README implies. Update public architecture docs to match the real layering and crate purposes discovered during codebase archaeology.","notes":"Updated README.md to reflect the real 12-crate workspace, current architecture layering, present project state, and agent-facing recommendation to run heavy conformance commands via rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:47.943967348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:40:08.406318013Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:40:08.405731714Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-uf4","title":"Align legacy oracle cartography wording in EXISTING_PYTORCH_STRUCTURE","description":"The top-level scope section in EXISTING_PYTORCH_STRUCTURE.md already treats legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch as a typical local mirror, but section 4 still shows the path as fixed. Update that line for consistency.","notes":"Made the legacy oracle cartography section consistent with the already-corrected scope wording by describing the local PyTorch mirror path as typical when present rather than fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:34:53.803252256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.784603527Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:35:11.783935432Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vidc","title":"ft-nn CELU forward severs autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs. Recent commits fixed SELU, Threshold, Hardtanh, Unfold, and Fold to compose through autograd-aware primitives, but CELU::forward at lines 3673-3690 still calls session.tensor_values(input), computes outputs in plain f64, and returns session.tensor_variable(output, input_shape, false). Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, call CELU::new(alpha).forward(...), reduce output with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for the CELU input will be absent/zero because the output is a fresh requires_grad=false leaf. This silently breaks training through CELU and violates the deterministic autograd contract. Fix: implement CELU via differentiable primitives, e.g. x_pos = relu(input), x_scaled = input / alpha, neg_branch = alpha * (exp(x_scaled) - 1) selected where input < 0, or an equivalent tensor_where/tensor_exp composition, and add a regression test asserting CELU input gradients match the analytical derivative.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T17:27:07.172932182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:48.010161720Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:48.007733332Z","close_reason":"CELU::forward now composes through tensor_where(input >= 0, input, alpha * (exp(input/alpha) - 1)) using tensor_div / tensor_mul / tensor_exp / tensor_sub primitives — all autograd-aware. Added celu_propagates_gradients regression test asserting analytical d CELU/dx (1 for x>=0, exp(x/alpha) for x<0) plus celu_rejects_zero_alpha guard.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-vjow","title":"Fix gaussian_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]","description":"gaussian_nll_loss at lib.rs:10398 computes:
  loss_i = 0.5 * (log(var_clamped_i) + (input_i - target_i)^2 / var_clamped_i)
plus optional 0.5 * log(2pi) constant.

Composable through tensor_clamp_min + tensor_log + tensor_sub + tensor_mul + tensor_div + scalar add for the optional constant. All three of input / target / var are differentiable in PyTorch's gaussian_nll_loss.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed gaussian_nll_loss through clamp_min/log/sub/mul/div/mean primitives. Added gaussian_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_var test (input=2, target=1, var=4 → grad_input = 0.25, grad_var = 0.09375).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:42:17.997902147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:53.567458970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:53.565164985Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vk5","title":"CPU round kernels use half-away instead of PyTorch half-even","description":"FrankenTorch CPU round parity bug. PyTorch torch.round uses round-half-to-even for ties, including round(2.5) -> 2 and round(-0.5) -> -0.0. The ft-kernel-cpu scalar and f64/f32 tensor round paths used Rust f64::round/f32::round, which round ties away from zero, so values like [-2.5, -0.5, 0.5, 2.5] diverged from PyTorch-observable semantics. Affected file: crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs. Fix: use round_ties_even for scalar, f64 tensor, and f32 tensor round kernels, and add explicit tie-even tests covering negative zero.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:32.708283716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:36.622304042Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:49:36.620543749Z","close_reason":"Implemented ft-kernel-cpu round_ties_even parity for scalar/f64/f32 paths and added tie-even tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vkk","title":"Audit UBS warnings in ft-api (unwrap/assert hotspots)","description":"UBS scan of crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs reported large counts of unwrap/expect and assert macros plus mutex lock expects. Triage which are test-only vs runtime paths, and replace reachable unwrap/assert with explicit error handling where needed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:17:29.032450794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:46:24.931782362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:46:24.931460446Z","close_reason":"Replaced runtime unwrap/expect paths in ft-api with explicit errors + overflow checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vl4","title":"Implement Unfold and Fold modules in ft-nn","description":"Add nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) modules. Critical for depthwise-separable convolutions and some attention patterns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:20.252832659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T06:08:09.280111854Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T06:08:09.279770839Z","close_reason":"Implemented nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) with padding/stride/dilation. 4 tests including roundtrip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
